channel.title: Bloomberg Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2025 BLOOMBERG L.P. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

item.title: Microsoft Asked FBI for Help Tracking Palestinian Protests
item.description: Twenty activists urging company to sever ties with Israeli military were arrested last week. Executive Brad Smith said he welcomed discussion but not disruption.
item.dc:creator: Matt Day, Brody Ford

media:content.media:description: Demonstrators hold Palestinian flags during a protest at the Microsoft Campus in Redmond, Washington, US, on Tuesday, Aug. 19, 2025. Microsoft Corp. employees have started setting up a protest encampment at the company’s Redmond, Washington, headquarters, ratcheting up a campaign calling for the company to stop doing business with Israel over its war in Gaza.

item.title: AT&T to Buy EchoStar Spectrum Licenses for About $23 Billion
item.description: AT&T Inc. is to buy spectrum licenses from EchoStar Corp. for about $23 billion in a deal that will expand the telecommunications company’s US network.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Solon

media:content.media:description: Fiber cables at the AT&T Inc. central office in Dallas, Texas. Photographer: Shelby Tauber/Bloomberg

item.title: Estonia Blames Russian Jamming for Explosion of Ukrainian Drone
item.description: An armed drone which crashed in Estonia is likely to be a Ukrainian drone that veered off-course due to Russia’s electronic interference in the Baltic region, officials in Tallinn said.
item.dc:creator: Ott Tammik

media:content.media:description: Hanno Pevkur

item.title: What Are the Digital Services Taxes Drawing Trump’s Ire?
item.description: Digital services taxes targeting the revenue of big technology companies have returned as a flash point in President Donald Trump’s efforts to rewrite the rules of global trade.
item.dc:creator: William Horobin

media:content.media:description: The Microsoft Teams app on a laptop

item.title: Sea Overtakes DBS as Southeast Asia’s Most Valuable Company
item.description: Singapore’s Sea Ltd. has reclaimed its title as Southeast Asia’s most valuable publicly traded company, surpassing DBS Group Holdings Ltd. after a 300% comeback rally powered by its e-commerce arm Shopee.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Poh

media:content.media:description: Packages at the sorting facility for Sea Ltd.'s logistics arm SPX Express in Singapore.

item.title: Baidu Mulls Another Dim Sum Bond Sale in Record Year for Market
item.description: Baidu Inc., China’s top search engine operator, is considering another dim sum bond offering just months after it priced its first such offshore yuan notes, people familiar with the matter said, amid a record year for the market.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Cai, Shuiyu Jing

media:content.media:description: The logo for Baidu Inc. Photographer: Andrea Verdelli/Bloomberg

item.title: Carlyle Agrees to Acquire Invesco Software Company Intelliflo
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. has agreed to acquire Invesco Ltd.-backed software maker intelliflo for as much as $200 million.
item.dc:creator: Swetha Gopinath

media:content.media:description: The Invesco Ltd. headquarters in Atlanta, Georgia. Photographer: Elijah Nouvelage/Bloomberg

item.title: Top Japan News Outlets Sue AI Startup Perplexity for Copyright Violations
item.description: Nikkei Inc. and the Asahi Shimbun Co. sued artificial-intelligence startup Perplexity AI Inc. for copyright infringement, joining other news organizations in Japan and the US that are challenging the use of their content in AI tools.
item.dc:creator: Mayumi Negishi

media:content.media:description: The Perplexity AI assistant app on a smartphone arranged in Riga, Latvia, on Wednesday, Aug. 13, 2025. AI startup Perplexity made a formal offer to acquire Google’s Chrome browser for $34.5 billion, an audacious bid to get ahead of a potential requirement for the search giant to sell the web browser in US antitrust proceedings.

item.title: AI Makes It Harder for Entry-Level Coders to Find Jobs, Study Says
item.description: Artificial intelligence is making it harder for entry-level workers in the US to find jobs in fields like software development and customer service, according to new research.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Metz

media:content.media:description: The ChatGPT virtual assistant app on a smartphone in Riga, Latvia, on Friday, Aug. 16, 2024. The public release of advanced generative AI tools such as Google's Gemini, Meta AI, and OpenAI's ChatGPT over the past two years has heightened fears that millions of workers could be displaced.

item.title: Illegal Sites Took 71% of Europe’s Betting, Casino Sales in 2024
item.description: Illegal gambling operators captured 71% of Europe’s online betting and casino market last year, or €80.65 billion ($93.8 billion), as regulators struggled to rein in crypto casinos, prediction markets and unlicensed sportsbooks during a booming year for sports.
item.dc:creator: Navya Menon

media:content.media:description: Spectator watch track and field competitions during the Paris 2024 Olympic Games at the Stade de France stadium in Paris, France, on Tuesday, Aug, 6, 2024. Forget the Hamptons, Nantucket or Capri — Wall Street is spending part of the summer at the Paris Olympics.

item.title: Bitcoin Nears Seven Week Low as Investors Shift Focus to Ether
item.description: Bitcoin sank to its lowest level in nearly seven weeks as the token retreats further from a record set in mid-August.
item.dc:creator: Sidhartha Shukla

media:content.media:description: A Bitcoin logo at a cryptocurrency exchange in Barcelona, Spain, on Wednesday, Aug. 13, 2025. Bitcoin and Ether are both trading within a whisker of all-time highs as investors pile into the two largest cryptocurrencies, with a recent wave of demand tilting in favor of the latter.

item.title: US Renewable Investments Fell 36% on Trump’s Policies, BNEF Says
item.description: US investments in renewable energy projects plunged this year as developers responded to White House policy shifts targeting the sector.
item.dc:creator: Tope Alake

media:content.media:description: The sun rises behind wind turbines. Photographer: Bloomberg Creative Photos/Bloomberg Creative Collection

item.title: Saudi’s Humain to Open Data Centers With US Chips in 2026
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s new artificial intelligence company, Humain, has broken ground on its first data centers in the kingdom and plans to have them up and running in early 2026 with the use of semiconductors imported from the US.
item.dc:creator: Christine Burke

media:content.media:description: Tareq Amin

item.title: Australian Online Retailer CEO Uses AI Voice to Present Results
item.description: When Kogan.com Ltd. founder and Chief Executive Officer Ruslan Kogan presented the online retailer’s results at its briefing on Monday, he decided to use an AI-generated version of his voice.
item.dc:creator: Aradhana Aravindan

media:content.media:description: MELBOURNE, AUSTRALIA - DECEMBER 14: Ruslan Kogan and Anastasia Fai Kogan attend the NGV Gala 2024 at the Opening of Yayoi Kusama Exhibition 2024 at the National Gallery of Victoria on December 14, 2024 in Melbourne, Australia. (Photo by Wendell Teodoro/Getty Images for NGV)

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Targeted Diplomats in Asia, Google Says
item.description: Diplomats in Southeast Asia were targeted in a cyber-espionage campaign earlier this year, likely waged in support of operations aligned with the strategic interests of China, according to Google.
item.dc:creator: Jane Lanhee Lee

item.title: GIC in Talks to Invest in Goodman’s Data Centers, AFR Says
item.description: Singapore’s sovereign wealth fund GIC is considering a proposal to invest in data center assets owned by Australian property firm Goodman Group, the Australian Financial Review reported.
item.dc:creator: Adam Haigh

media:content.media:description: An artist rendering of the Goodman LAX01 Vernon (LAX01) data center in Vernon, California.

item.title: Family Office to Billionaire Murthy Warns on Startup Valuations
item.description: The family office of tech billionaire Narayana Murthy is growing cautious on startups in India, pointing to steep discounts driven by funds that need to exit their investments.
item.dc:creator: Advait Palepu

media:content.media:description: Deepak Padaki

item.title: Musk Sues Apple, OpenAI, Saying They Hurt AI Competition
item.description: Elon Musk accused Apple Inc. and OpenAI in a lawsuit of unfairly favoring the artificial intelligence company across iPhones and thwarting competition for other chatbot makers.
item.dc:creator: Madlin Mekelburg

media:content.media:description: Elon Musk

item.title: Thoma Bravo Lines Up $2.7 Billion of Bank Debt for Verint Buyout
item.description: Banco Santander SA is leading a roughly $2.7 billion debt deal to support Thoma Bravo’s acquisition of customer-service automation business Verint Systems Inc., according to people with knowledge of the matter.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Weinman, Gowri Gurumurthy

media:content.media:description: Signage at the Thoma Bravo private equity firm in the Brickell neighborhood of Miami, Florida, US, on Wednesday, March 5, 2025. Since the current iteration of Thoma Bravo was founded in 2008, it's been involved in roughly a fifth of all private equity buyouts in the sector as measured by value, according to data compiled by Bloomberg. Photographer: Scott McIntyre/Bloomberg

item.title: Japan’s Niche Chip Gear Makers Left Out of Nvidia Boom, CEO Says
item.description: Prices are rising across Japan, but not for the components of chipmaking gear. Tokyo Electron Ltd. supplier Marumae Co. is seeking out deals to change that.
item.dc:creator: Takashi Mochizuki, Yuki Furukawa

media:content.media:description: Marumae's electron beam welding equipment. Source: Marumae Co.

item.title: Interactive Brokers Climbs on Replacing Walgreens in S&P 500
item.description: (Updates for postmarket share move, adds Talen Energy.)
item.dc:creator: Norah Mulinda

media:content.media:description: The Interactive Brokers Group application login screen on a smartphone arranged in Dobbs Ferry, New York, U.S., on Sunday, Jan. 16, 2022. Interactive Brokers Group is scheduled to release earns on January 18.

item.title: Elon Musk Sues Apple, US Takes Intel Stake | Bloomberg Tech 8/25/2025
item.description: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde discusses the latest on Intel after President Donald Trump announced a 10% stake in the chipmaker. Plus, Elon Musk announces he's suing Apple and OpenAI, accusing the companies of thwarting competition. And the world of esports is set to get bigger with the introduction of the Esports Nations Cup tournament. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: El iPhone se renueva: Apple lanza nuevo modelo Air y planea su primer plegable para 2026
item.description: Tras años de rediseños mínimos, Apple inicia una nueva era.
item.dc:creator: Mark Gurman

media:content.media:description: Apple Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook unveils the iPhone X.

item.title: Andreessen Horowitz Joins $100 Million Effort to Shape AI Regulation
item.description: Venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz is joining other major Silicon Valley investors in backing a new $100 million political network that seeks to influence US artificial intelligence policy, deepening the tech industry’s involvement in politics under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Kate Clark

media:content.media:description: Tech industry artificial intelligence advocates have said that excessive regulation would kneecap a critical industry.

item.title: AirTrunk Mulls Bond Sales After Refinancing $10 Billion of Loans
item.description: AirTrunk Pte has closed an A$16 billion ($10.4 billion) refinancing, the largest sustainability-linked financing ever in Asia Pacific and Japan and now is looking for ways to raise new capital in one of the hottest corners of credit markets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Klyne

media:content.media:description: An AirTrunk data center in Sydney.

item.title: Shopify Revives a ‘Hateful Content’ Ban Amid Sales of Racist Merchandise
item.description: Shopify Inc. tightened rules against “hateful content” as users sell racist merchandise using its popular e-commerce platform, roughly a year after removing a similar ban.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Seal

item.title: AI Disruption Fear Sparks Investor Scrutiny of Software Stocks
item.description: For years, software companies were the toast of Wall Street. High profit margins, low capital requirements and vast runway for growth prompted the venture capitalist Marc Andreessen in 2011 to famously declare “software is eating the world.”
item.dc:creator: Jeran Wittenstein, Ryan Vlastelica

item.title: Thoma Bravo to Buy Verint for $1.23 Billion in Cash Deal
item.description: Thoma Bravo has agreed to buy Verint Systems Inc. for $1.23 billion in cash, just days after announcing a $12.3 billion takeover of Dayforce Inc.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Gould

item.title: Biotechs Turn to Digital Coins, Crypto to Boost Stock Prices
item.description: They’re supposed to be on the cutting edge of medicine. Yet some tiny biotech companies, largely left behind in the stock market’s breakneck rally, are pursuing a new cure-all: Cryptocurrencies.
item.dc:creator: Monique Mulima, Angel Adegbesan

item.title: Malaysia Unveils First AI Device Chip to Join Global Race
item.description: Malaysia unveiled its own AI processor Monday, joining a global race to build the most sought-after electronic components for artificial intelligence development.
item.dc:creator: Yuan Gao, Mackenzie Hawkins
